LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT 24 HOUR FITNESS USA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH CAUSES OF ACTION 1-3 (PRODUCTS LIABILITY). THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATES THAT DEFENDANT IS IN THE BUSINESS OF PROVIDING FITNESS SERVICES AND THE SQUAT MACHINE WAS AN INCIDENT TO THOSE SERVICES. SEE ONTIVEROS V. 24 HOUR FITNESS (2008) 169 CAL.APP.4TH 424. THE DOCTRINE OF PRIMARY ASSUMPTION OF THE RISK BARS CAUSES OF ACTION 4 AND 5. DEFENDANTS OWED NO LEGAL DUTY TO ELIMINATE (OR PROTECT A PLAINTIFF AGAINST) RISKS INHERENT IN THE ACTIVITY. DEFENDANT DID NOTHING TO INCREASE THE DANGER AND THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF GROSS NEGLIGENCE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF EGNACIO BALINTON'S MOTION FOR ISSUE SANCTIONS TO PRECLUDE EVIDENCE, ARGUMENT AND ISSUES AGAINST DEFENDANTS KNIGHT OIL TOOLS LLC, RAYNE PROPERTIES LLC, AND ROBINSON TUBULAR SERVICES INC., DUE TO FAILURE TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS AND EVIDENCE IN VIOLATION OF COURT ORDER, AND DISCOVERY REQUESTS & REQUESTS FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS CCP 2031.320 IS DENIED. Off Calendar Nov-19-2013 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-31-2013 Continued To Jan-07-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant 24 Hour Fitness Usa, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-25-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LAWRENCE D. MURRAY, TEL. NO. 673-0555 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BRUCE L. DAVIS OF THE PRINDLE, AMARO, GOETZ, HILLYARD & REINHOLTZ, TEL. NO. (562) 436-3946 X 138 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT 24 HOUR FITNESS USA BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. JOHN OMERA OF THE BREMER WHYTE & O'MEARA LLP, TEL. NO. (818) 712-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant 24 Hour Fitness USA, Inc.'s Motion For Summary Judgment is granted. Plaintiff cannot establish causes of action 1-3 (products liability). The undisputed evidence demonstrates that defendant is in the business of providing fitness services and the squat machine was an incident to those services. See Ontiveros v. 24 Hour Fitness (2008) 169 Cal.App.4th 424. The doctrine of primary assumption of the risk bars causes of action 4 and 5. Defendants owed no legal duty to eliminate (or protect a plaintiff against) risks inherent in the activity. Defendant did nothing to increase the danger and there is no evidence of gross negligence. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)

ATTY JOHN O'MEARA, BREMER WHYTE BROWN & O'MEARA, 818.712.9800, APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT KNIGHT OIL TOOLS BY COURT CALL.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF EGNACIO BALINTON'S Motion For Issue Sanctions To Preclude Evidence, Argument And Issues Against Defendants Knight Oil Tools LLC, Rayne Properties LLC, and Robinson Tubular Services Inc., Due To Failure To Produce Documents And Evidence In Violation Of Court Order, And Discovery Requests & Requests For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs CCP 2031.320 is Denied. Mr. Balinton has not provided designated facts for the Court to consider the issue sanctions. (CCP 2023.030(b).) Further, Defendants have not engaged in a misuse of the discovery process.
